details: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Bal Hridaya Upchar Yojana" was launched by the Public Health and Family Welfare Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh. The scheme aims to provide free treatment and surgery to children aged 0 to 15 years who are suffering from identified heart diseases. The scheme provides financial assistance ranging from ₹25,000/- to ₹1,00,000/- for treatment, covering 07 identified heart diseases in children. Key features include covering costs for treatment at recognized government or private hospitals and ensuring the process is governed by the Madhya Pradesh Public Services Guarantee Act, 2010.
benefits: Financial assistance ranging from ₹25,000/- to ₹1,00,000/- per case for treatment.
Provision of free treatment/surgery for 07 identified heart diseases in children.
The sanctioned amount is transferred to the account of the concerned medical institution via Cheque or E-Payment (e-transfer).
The benefit delivery period is stipulated as 20 working days.
Treatment must be confined to the approved package amount.
Hospitals must not demand any additional fee from the patient for treatment beyond the sanctioned amount.
The beneficiary or hospital must submit a detailed expenditure report/utilization certificate for the funds spent on the patient's treatment.
A duplicate or photocopy of the discharge ticket issued after the patient's recovery must be provided.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Madhya Pradesh.
The applicant must be a member of an Economically Weaker Family or a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.
The applicant must ensure their name is recorded in the family roster and they hold a ration card.
The intended beneficiary (child) must be aged 0 to 15 years.
The intended beneficiary (child) must be suffering from one of the identified heart diseases.

offline: Madhya Pradesh Lok Sewa Guarantee Portal
In the registration page, provide the following mandatory information: Name, Gender, Mobile No., Email ID.
Create a new password and confirm the same. Fill the captcha code. Verify your Mobile Number via OTP. Finally, click "Register".
Login using your registered Email ID and the password created during registration.

On the homepage, sort the list of services "By Department". Further, select the Department and the respective scheme that you intend to apply for. On the next page, detailed information about the scheme will be displayed, along with the following resources:
of the Application Form (For Offline Application).
Link to Apply Online (By Self or Through Lok Sewa Kendra / MP Online / CSC).

In the scheme's online application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size (fields marked with * are mandatory), and click "Submit".
Alternatively, you may apply via your nearest Lok Sewa Kendra / MP Online / CSC by making a payment of ₹20/-.
The applicant may file an appeal in the following situations:
Non-receipt of the application acknowledgment receipt.
Approved treatment amount is less than the prescribed package/estimate.
Non-resolution within the specified time limit.
Must be filed with the Collector. The prescribed time limit for the resolution of the first appeal is 15 working days.
Must be filed with the Samagra Sanyukt Sanchalak, Swasthya Seva (Integrated Joint Director, Health Services).

documents_required: Estimate From A Government Or Government-Recognized Private Hospital (Prāklan/Estimate).
Proof Of Being Economically Weaker Section Or Below Poverty Line (Family List/Ration Card).
Residence Proof (For Rural Area: Certificate Issued By Chief Executive Officer, Janpad Panchayat; For Urban Area: Certificate Issued By Commissioner, Nagar Palika/Nagar Parishad Official).
Recent Color Photograph Of The Patient.
Patient's Age Proof (Birth Certificate/Aadhar Card/Panchayat Certificate/Any Other Authorized Document).
Duplicate Or Photocopy Of The Discharge Ticket Issued After Treatment.
sources_and_references: Locate Your Nearest Lok Sewa Kendra

faqs.question: What is the maximum financial assistance amount that can be provided for the treatment of a child suffering from heart disease under this government program?
faqs.answer: The financial assistance provided ranges from a minimum of ₹25,000/- to a maximum of ₹1,00,000/- per case, depending on the specific heart condition and treatment requirements.

faqs.question: If my child is 16 years old and has been diagnosed with a heart disease covered under this scheme, will they be eligible to receive the benefits?
faqs.answer: No, the intended beneficiary must be aged between 0 to 15 years at the time of application. Children who have crossed 15 years of age are not eligible for benefits under this program.

faqs.question: Are there any restrictions on the type of medical institutions where my child can receive treatment under this financial assistance program for heart diseases?
faqs.answer: Yes, the treatment must be conducted at government hospitals or government-recognized private hospitals only. The medical institution must be approved under the scheme to provide treatment for the identified heart diseases.

faqs.question: What happens if the hospital where my child is receiving treatment demands additional payment beyond the sanctioned amount that was approved under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Hospitals are strictly prohibited from demanding any additional fees from patients beyond the sanctioned amount approved under the scheme. Treatment costs must be confined to the approved package amount only.

faqs.question: Is it necessary for my family to possess a ration card and have our names recorded in the family roster to qualify for this heart treatment assistance?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is mandatory that the applicant's name is recorded in the family roster and the family holds a valid ration card as part of the eligibility verification process.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take from the date of application approval to the actual disbursement of the financial assistance to the medical institution for treatment?
faqs.answer: The benefit delivery period is stipulated as 20 working days from the date of approval. The sanctioned amount is transferred to the medical institution's account via Cheque or Electronic Payment.

faqs.question: What kind of proof do I need to submit to establish that my family belongs to the Economically Weaker Section or Below Poverty Line category?
faqs.answer: You must submit your family list or ration card as proof of belonging to the Economically Weaker Section or Below Poverty Line category at the time of application.

faqs.question: Can I still receive financial assistance if my child has already undergone treatment and surgery for the heart disease before receiving official approval under this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, the beneficiary is not eligible for assistance if treatment for the sanctioned disease was sought prior to the approval date. Treatment must be undertaken only after receiving official approval.

faqs.question: What are the different documents that must be submitted by the hospital or the family after the child's treatment has been completed and they have been discharged?
faqs.answer: After treatment completion, a detailed expenditure report or utilization certificate for the funds spent must be submitted, along with a duplicate or photocopy of the discharge ticket issued after the patient's recovery.

faqs.question: How many specific types of heart diseases are covered under this financial assistance scheme for children in Madhya Pradesh, and what is the age criterion?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides coverage for 07 identified heart diseases in children. The intended beneficiary must be aged between 0 to 15 years and suffering from one of these identified conditions.

faqs.question: What type of residence proof is required for families living in rural areas versus those living in urban areas when applying for this heart treatment assistance?
faqs.answer: For rural areas, a certificate issued by the Chief Executive Officer of the Janpad Panchayat is required. For urban areas, a certificate issued by the Commissioner of Nagar Palika or Nagar Parishad is necessary.

faqs.question: Is there any application fee that needs to be paid when submitting the application through Lok Sewa Kendra, MP Online, or Common Service Centre?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you choose to apply through your nearest Lok Sewa Kendra, MP Online, or Common Service Centre, a payment of ₹20/- is required for processing the application.

faqs.question: What documents are acceptable as valid proof of the child's age when submitting an application for financial assistance under this heart treatment program?
faqs.answer: Acceptable age proof documents include Birth Certificate, Aadhar Card, Panchayat Certificate, or any other authorized document that officially establishes the child's date of birth and current age.

faqs.question: To whom should I submit my offline application form along with all the required documents if I choose not to apply through the online portal?
faqs.answer: The duly filled and signed application form, along with all mandatory documents, should be submitted to the Chief Medical and Health Officer of your district within the prescribed period.
